Steps: 1. Open browser and navigate to /login | 2. Enter the admin email address (SUPER_ADMIN account) | 3. Enter the correct password | 4. Click the Sign In button
Expected Result: Redirected to /admin dashboard. Admin navigation sidebar visible. No error message shown. JWT token stored in sessionStorage.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter a registered organizer's email | 3. Enter the correct password | 4. Click Sign In
Expected Result: Redirected to /organizer/dashboard. Organizer navigation visible with greeting message. Dashboard summary cards load with tournament/revenue data.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter a valid registered email | 3. Enter an incorrect password | 4. Click Sign In
Expected Result: Error message displayed (e.g. 'Invalid credentials'). User remains on /login page. No redirect.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter an email address that does not exist in the system | 3. Enter any password | 4. Click Sign In
Expected Result: Error message displayed. User stays on /login. No crash or server error exposed.

Steps: 1. Ensure you are logged out (open an incognito window or clear sessionStorage) | 2. Navigate directly to /admin
Expected Result: Redirected to /login page. Admin dashboard content not shown.

Steps: 1. Ensure you are logged out | 2. Navigate directly to /organizer/dashboard
Expected Result: Redirected to /login page. Dashboard content not visible.

Steps: 1. Log in with admin credentials | 2. Navigate directly to /organizer/dashboard
Expected Result: Redirected away from /organizer/dashboard (to / or /admin). Organizer-specific content not accessible to admin role.

Steps: 1. Log in with organizer credentials | 2. Navigate directly to /admin
Expected Result: Redirected away from /admin (to / or /organizer/dashboard). Admin panel content not accessible to organizer role.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Click the Logout button in the navigation | 4. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard again
Expected Result: Redirected to /login after logout. Re-navigating to /organizer/dashboard while logged out redirects back to /login.

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Click the Logout button in the admin navigation | 4. Navigate to /admin again
Expected Result: Redirected to /login after logout. Admin session cleared. Re-navigating to /admin redirects to /login.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard (ensure page has loaded fully) | 3. Click Logout | 4. Immediately press the browser Back button
Expected Result: User does NOT see the dashboard again. Either stays on /login or is redirected back to /login. Stale session data not shown.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Wait for all content to load | 4. Press F5 (or Cmd+R) to hard-refresh | 5. Observe the page after reload
Expected Result: Page reloads and user remains logged in. Dashboard content loads normally. No redirect to /login. No infinite loading skeleton.

Steps: 1. Open a new browser tab | 2. Navigate to / (home page) | 3. Observe the page content
Expected Result: Page renders within 3 seconds. Tournament cards are displayed. Each card shows tournament title, date, venue, organizer name, and a Register or View button. If no tournaments exist a friendly empty state is shown.

Steps: 1. Navigate to / | 2. Click on any tournament card | 3. Observe the detail page at /tournaments/[id]
Expected Result: Tournament detail page loads. Shows: tournament title, organizer name, banner/poster (if uploaded), info cards (dates, venue, deadline), description, tabs for Categories and Participants. Register buttons visible per category (if deadline not passed and seats available).

Feature: Register for a tournament — full flow
Steps: 1. Navigate to /tournaments/[id] for a tournament with open registration | 2. Click Register on a category | 3. On the registration form fill in: Player Name, Phone Number, and optionally Email and City | 4. Enter a FIDE ID if applicable | 5. Click Submit | 6. If entry fee > 0 complete the Razorpay payment flow | 7. Observe the confirmation
Expected Result: Registration created. Confirmation page shown at /registration/[entryNumber] with the player's entry number, category, payment status CONFIRMED, and tournament details.

Steps: 1. Complete a registration and note the entry number (e.g. ECA-2026-0001) | 2. Open a new tab | 3. Navigate to /registration/ECA-2026-0001
Expected Result: Page loads and displays: entry number, player name, tournament title, category name, registration date, payment status. No authentication required.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /registration/INVALID-XYZ-9999
Expected Result: Friendly 'not found' or error page displayed. No server stack trace exposed. HTTP 404 or equivalent error state shown.

Feature: FIDE ID lookup in registration form
Steps: 1. Navigate to /tournaments/[id]/register?category=[categoryId] | 2. Locate the FIDE ID input field | 3. Enter a known valid FIDE ID (e.g. a real FIDE ID from fide.com) | 4. Tab out of or blur the field
Expected Result: FIDE data retrieved: player's FIDE rating is auto-populated or a 'FIDE verified' indicator appears next to the field. If FIDE ID is invalid or not found, an appropriate error or 'not found' message is shown inline.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Observe the four summary stat cards
Expected Result: Four cards visible: Total Tournaments, Active Tournaments, Total Registrations, Total Revenue (₹). Values match the actual data for this organizer. Revenue change percentage (vs last month) shown. Page fully loads within 3 seconds.

Steps: 1. Ensure at least one tournament exists in DRAFT status for this organizer | 2. Log in as organizer | 3. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard
Expected Result: Yellow/orange attention banner displayed near the top of the dashboard with a message indicating there is a tournament awaiting submission. A 'View pending' button links to the tournaments list or tournament detail.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Click the '+ Create Tournament' button (or equivalent CTA in the dashboard)
Expected Result: Navigates to /organizer/tournaments/new using Next.js client-side navigation (no full page reload). The create tournament form loads immediately without a blank intermediate state.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Find the Your Tournaments section | 4. Click the 'View all' link
Expected Result: Navigates to /organizer/tournaments. The full paginated list of the organizer's tournaments loads.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer (ensure at least one registration exists for their tournament) | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Locate the Recent Registrations section
Expected Result: Up to 5 most recent registrations listed. Each entry shows player name, category, payment status badge (CONFIRMED / PENDING), and registration timestamp.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer (ensure at least one APPROVED or ACTIVE tournament with a future start date exists) | 2. Navigate to /organizer/dashboard | 3. Locate the Upcoming Tournaments section
Expected Result: Up to 3 upcoming tournaments listed. Each shows tournament title, start date, days-until countdown, and confirmed registration count.

Feature: Create new tournament with all fields
Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments/new | 3. Fill Title: 'Test Open 2026' | 4. Fill Description: any text | 5. Fill City: 'Chennai' | 6. Fill Venue: 'Chess Club Hall' | 7. Set Start Date: any future date | 8. Set End Date: same or later than start | 9. Set Registration Deadline: before start date | 10. In Categories section: enter Name 'Open', Min Age 0, Max Age 99, Entry Fee 50000 (paise = ₹500), Max Seats 64 | 11. Optionally upload a poster image (JPG/PNG under 5MB) | 12. Click Create Tournament
Expected Result: Tournament created and saved with DRAFT status. Redirected to dashboard or tournament list. Tournament appears in the list. If poster uploaded it is stored and shown.

Feature: Create tournament with missing required fields
Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments/new | 3. Leave the Title field empty | 4. Fill all other required fields | 5. Click Create Tournament
Expected Result: Validation error displayed for the Title field. Form is not submitted. User stays on the create page. No tournament created in the database.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments | 3. Click Registrations or detail action for a DRAFT tournament | 4. On the registrations page find and click the 'Submit for Approval' button | 5. Confirm if prompted
Expected Result: Tournament status changes from DRAFT to PENDING_APPROVAL. Status badge updates in the list. The submit button is replaced or disabled. Admin can now see the tournament in their pending queue.

Feature: View tournament list with all statuses
Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments | 3. Scroll through the list
Expected Result: All tournaments displayed regardless of status. Status badge shown per row (DRAFT / PENDING_APPROVAL / APPROVED / ACTIVE / CLOSED). Correct colour coding per status.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments | 3. Type part of a tournament title in the search input | 4. Clear the search input
Expected Result: Table filters in real-time (or on Enter) to show only tournaments whose title matches the search query. Clearing the search restores the full list.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments | 3. Click the Status filter dropdown | 4. Select 'DRAFT' | 5. Then select 'PENDING_APPROVAL'
Expected Result: Table shows only tournaments matching the selected status. Switching filter immediately updates the list. Selecting no filter (clear) restores all tournaments.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer (ensure more than 10 tournaments exist) | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments | 3. Click the Next page (›) button | 4. Click Previous (‹) | 5. Change page size from 10 to 25 in the page size selector
Expected Result: Next/previous navigation loads correct data slices. Page indicator updates. Changing page size resets to page 1 and shows the correct number of rows per page.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments | 3. Click the Registrations link for any tournament | 4. Observe the page at /organizer/tournaments/[id]/registrations
Expected Result: Registrations table loads. Columns visible: Entry #, Player, Category, Status (CONFIRMED / PENDING), Phone, City, Registered date. Stat cards show total, confirmed, and pending counts.

Steps: 1. Navigate to the registrations page for a tournament | 2. Type part of a player's name in the search box | 3. Clear the search
Expected Result: Table filters to show only rows matching the search query across player name, entry number, and other searchable fields. Clearing restores all registrations.

Steps: 1. Navigate to the registrations page | 2. Click the 'Player' column header | 3. Click again to reverse sort | 4. Click 'Entry #' column header
Expected Result: Rows reorder based on the clicked column. Sort direction arrow icon appears and toggles direction on second click.

Steps: 1. Navigate to a tournament with more than 10 registrations | 2. Click Next page | 3. Change page size to 25 using the selector
Expected Result: Correct registrations shown per page. Page 2 shows rows 11-20 (default). Changing page size resets to page 1 and shows the configured number of rows.

Steps: 1. Navigate to the registrations page for a tournament | 2. Look for an Export or Download CSV button | 3. Click it
Expected Result: A CSV or Excel file is downloaded containing all registrations for the tournament. File includes columns: Entry #, Player Name, Category, Status, Phone, Email, City, FIDE ID, Registered At.

Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. Navigate to /organizer/tournaments/[id]/registrations for a tournament | 3. Scroll to the Chess Results / Live Data section | 4. Enter a valid chess-results.com URL (e.g. https://s2.chess-results.com/tnr1381175.aspx?lan=1) | 5. Optionally select a category from the dropdown | 6. Click Add / Link
Expected Result: Link is created and appears in the list with syncStatus = PENDING. A background sync is triggered. After the sync completes (within ~2 minutes) status changes to ACTIVE.

Steps: 1. Navigate to the Chess Results section on a tournament page | 2. Enter an invalid URL such as: 'https://google.com' or 'not-a-url' | 3. Click Add
Expected Result: Error message shown: 'Invalid chess-results.com URL' or similar. No link created. The form input is highlighted or an inline error displayed.

Feature: Re-sync button triggers fresh data fetch
Steps: 1. Navigate to a tournament that has an ACTIVE chess-results link | 2. Note the 'Last synced' timestamp | 3. Click the Sync / Re-sync button
Expected Result: SyncStatus briefly changes to SYNCING. After completion (seconds to minutes depending on tournament size) status returns to ACTIVE and the Last Synced timestamp is updated.

Steps: 1. Add a new valid chess-results.com URL to a tournament | 2. Immediately observe the status (should be PENDING) | 3. Wait up to 15 minutes or click Re-sync | 4. Refresh the page
Expected Result: Status transitions: PENDING → SYNCING → ACTIVE. lastSyncedAt field is populated with a recent timestamp. No ERROR status shown.

Steps: 1. Navigate to a tournament with an active chess-results link | 2. Click the Remove / Delete (trash icon) button next to the link | 3. Confirm the deletion if prompted | 4. Navigate to the public tournament page /tournaments/[id]
Expected Result: Link removed from the list. On the public tournament detail page the Live tab no longer appears (after a page refresh).

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin
Expected Result: Analytics cards display: Total Tournaments, Active Tournaments, Verified Organizers, Total Registrations, Platform Revenue. All values are populated. Loading states resolve correctly.

Steps: 1. Ensure at least one tournament has been submitted for approval (status = PENDING_APPROVAL) | 2. Log in as admin | 3. Navigate to /admin | 4. Scroll to the Pending Approvals / pending tournaments section
Expected Result: Tournaments with PENDING_APPROVAL status are listed with their title, organizer name, and Approve/Reject action buttons.

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Find a PENDING_APPROVAL tournament in the pending section | 4. Click the Approve button
Expected Result: Tournament status changes to APPROVED. Action button shows 'Done'. The tournament will now appear as APPROVED in the organizer's list.

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Find a PENDING_APPROVAL tournament | 4. Click the Reject button | 5. In the prompt that appears enter a reason (e.g. 'Missing category details') | 6. Click OK
Expected Result: Tournament status changes to REJECTED. Action button shows 'Done'. Rejection reason is stored.

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Scroll to the Organizers section | 4. Find an organizer with status PENDING_VERIFICATION | 5. Click the Verify button
Expected Result: Organizer status changes to ACTIVE. The button is replaced with an 'Active' badge. The organizer can now create and manage tournaments.

Feature: Data integrity check panel opens and runs
Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Scroll to the Data Integrity section | 4. Click 'Run Integrity Check' (or the equivalent button)
Expected Result: Integrity check panel expands or opens. Results are fetched from the API. Individual checks are listed (e.g. seat count consistency, payment integrity) each with a PASS or FAIL indicator.

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Click 'Audit Logs' in the admin navigation sidebar
Expected Result: Navigates to /admin/audit-logs using client-side navigation (no full page reload). Audit log table is loaded and displayed.

Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin/tournaments
Expected Result: All tournaments across all organizers are listed. Stat cards show Total, Active, and Pending counts. Each row shows: tournament name, city, organizer, status badge, category count, start date, and approve/reject actions for pending ones.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/tournaments | 2. Click the Status filter | 3. Select 'PENDING_APPROVAL' | 4. Verify results | 5. Select 'ACTIVE' | 6. Clear the filter
Expected Result: Table shows only tournaments matching the selected status. Filter changes immediately update the visible rows. Clearing the filter shows all tournaments again.

Feature: Search by tournament name or organizer
Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/tournaments | 2. Type part of a tournament title in the search box | 3. Type a city name | 4. Clear the search
Expected Result: Table filters to show matching tournaments. Search matches against title, organizer name, and city. Clearing the search restores all results.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/tournaments | 2. Apply filter Status = PENDING_APPROVAL to find a pending tournament | 3. Click the Approve button in that row
Expected Result: Tournament status updates to APPROVED. The row's action cell changes to 'Done'. No full-page reload required.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/tournaments | 2. Find a PENDING_APPROVAL tournament | 3. Click the Reject button | 4. Enter a rejection reason in the browser prompt | 5. Click OK
Expected Result: Tournament status changes to REJECTED. The action cell shows 'Done'. Rejection reason is persisted.

Feature: All organizers listed with correct info
Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin/organizers
Expected Result: All registered organizers displayed. Stat cards show Total, Verified, and Pending counts. Each row shows: initials avatar, academy name, city, email, phone, registration date, and status (Active badge or Verify button).

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/organizers | 2. Click the Status filter | 3. Select 'Pending' | 4. Then select 'Active'
Expected Result: Table updates to show only organizers matching the selected status (PENDING_VERIFICATION for Pending, ACTIVE for Active). Filtering is applied client-side on the current page.

Feature: Search organizers by name / email / city
Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/organizers | 2. Type an academy name in the search box | 3. Type an email address | 4. Type a city
Expected Result: Table filters to matching organizers. Search applies across academy name, email, city, and phone number.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/organizers | 2. Find an organizer showing the 'Verify' button (PENDING_VERIFICATION) | 3. Click Verify | 4. Observe result
Expected Result: Organizer status changes to ACTIVE immediately. The Verify button is replaced with the 'Active' badge. Stat card counters update.

Feature: Audit logs page loads with data
Steps: 1. Log in as admin | 2. Navigate to /admin/audit-logs
Expected Result: Audit log table loads. Visible columns: Timestamp (IST), Action badge (CREATE / UPDATE / DELETE / APPROVE etc.), Entity type and ID, Changes (old→new values), Performed By (email and role). Entries sorted by most recent first.

Feature: Search audit logs by action or email
Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/audit-logs | 2. Type 'APPROVE' in the search box | 3. Type an organizer's email address
Expected Result: Table filters to show only log entries whose action or performer email matches the search query. Note: search is applied client-side on the currently loaded page.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/audit-logs | 2. Click the Entity Type filter | 3. Select 'tournament' | 4. Select 'organizer' | 5. Select 'registration' | 6. Clear the filter
Expected Result: Table shows only audit entries for the selected entity type. Switching between entity types immediately updates the list. Clearing the filter shows all entity types.

Steps: 1. Navigate to /admin/audit-logs (ensure more than 10 log entries exist) | 2. Click the Next page button | 3. Click Previous page | 4. Change page size from 10 to 25
Expected Result: Each page loads a different set of audit entries via cursor-based pagination. Previous page returns to the previous set. Changing page size resets to page 1 with the new row count.

Feature: Live tab appears for tournament with active chess-results link
Steps: 1. Ensure a tournament has a chess-results link with syncStatus = ACTIVE in the database | 2. Navigate to the public tournament detail page /tournaments/[id] | 3. Observe the tab bar
Expected Result: Three tabs visible: '🏷 Categories & Seats', '👥 Participants', and '📡 Live'. The Live tab is clickable and loads live data.

Feature: Live tab hidden for tournament without chess-results link
Steps: 1. Navigate to /tournaments/[id] for a tournament that has NO chess-results links (or all links are DISABLED/ERROR) | 2. Observe the tab bar
Expected Result: Only two tabs visible: '🏷 Categories & Seats' and '👥 Participants'. The '📡 Live' tab is absent.

Steps: 1. Navigate to a tournament with ACTIVE chess-results link | 2. Click the '📡 Live' tab | 3. Click Round 1 button | 4. Note the standings/pairings shown | 5. Click Round 2 (if available) | 6. Click Round 3
Expected Result: Table data updates each time a different round is selected. The selected round button is highlighted (primary style). Data matches the round's actual pairings or standings from chess-results.com.

Steps: 1. Click the '📡 Live' tab on a tournament with synced data | 2. Ensure 'Standings' view is selected | 3. Observe the standings table for Round 1
Expected Result: Standings table shows columns: Rank, Name, Rating, Points. Player names are correct full names (not sequential numbers). Points show 1 for wins and 0.5 for draws. Top 3 ranks have gold-coloured rank numbers. Players with 0 rating show '—'.

Steps: 1. Click the '📡 Live' tab | 2. Click the 'Pairings' button | 3. Select Round 1
Expected Result: Pairings table shows columns: Bd (board number), White (player name), Rtg, Result, Rtg, Black (player name). Results display as '1 - 0', '0 - 1', or '½ - ½'. Unrated players show '—' in Rtg column. Bye entries handled gracefully.

Feature: Switch between Standings and Pairings views
Steps: 1. Open the Live tab | 2. Verify Standings is the default view | 3. Click the 'Pairings' button | 4. Click the 'Standings' button | 5. Repeat toggle several times
Expected Result: Seamless toggle between the two views. No page reload or navigation. The active view button shows primary style. Data for the selected view loads correctly each time.

Feature: Browser back button works without blank page
Steps: 1. Open browser and navigate to / | 2. Click on any tournament card to go to /tournaments/[id] | 3. Press the browser Back button | 4. For organizer: log in and navigate from /organizer/dashboard to /organizer/tournaments, then press Back | 5. For admin: navigate from /admin to /admin/tournaments, then press Back
Expected Result: Back navigation returns to the previous page with content fully loaded. No blank white page. No stuck loading skeleton. Content appears within 1 second of back navigation.

Feature: No stuck loading skeletons on any page
Steps: 1. Open DevTools network tab | 2. Visit each of these pages and navigate away and back: /, /tournaments/[id], /login, /organizer/dashboard, /organizer/tournaments, /organizer/tournaments/[id]/registrations, /admin, /admin/tournaments, /admin/organizers, /admin/audit-logs | 3. On each page navigate away and return using the browser Back button
Expected Result: All pages fully hydrate and replace loading skeleton with real content after returning via browser Back. No page remains in an indefinite skeleton loading state. Content loads within 3 seconds.

Feature: No console errors on key pages
Steps: 1. Open browser DevTools (F12) and click the Console tab | 2. Clear the console | 3. Navigate to / | 4. Repeat for /tournaments/[id], /login, /organizer/dashboard, /admin
Expected Result: No red ERROR messages appear in the console on any page. Warning messages are acceptable. React hydration errors or unhandled promise rejections should not be present.

Feature: All Next.js Link components navigate without page reload
Steps: 1. Log in as organizer | 2. On /organizer/dashboard click '+ Create Tournament' | 3. Press Back | 4. Click 'View all' tournaments link | 5. Press Back | 6. On public home page click a tournament card | 7. Click '← All tournaments' back link
Expected Result: All navigations use Next.js client-side routing (no full browser page reload — check the network tab: no document request). URL changes instantly and content loads without white flash.

Steps: 1. Open browser DevTools (F12) | 2. Toggle device toolbar | 3. Set viewport to 375 × 667 (iPhone SE) | 4. Navigate to: /, /tournaments/[id], /login, /organizer/dashboard, /admin, /admin/organizers | 5. On each page scroll vertically and check for horizontal overflow
Expected Result: No horizontal scrollbar visible on any page at 375px width. All text, buttons, and cards fit within the viewport. Tables scroll horizontally within their own container (table-wrapper) without causing page-level overflow. Form inputs are full-width and usable on mobile.
